title: pelargonium × gruinale
intro: Pelargonium × gruinale, also known as Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, characterized by its distinctive flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in South Africa, where it is native, to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × gruinale include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate cold symptoms. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
